name: Pillars Fund
mission: Pillars Fund amplifies the leadership, narratives, and talents of Muslims in the United States to advance opportunity and justice for all.
theoryOfChange: Tapping into Muslim storytelling to transform society and challenge stereotypes through authentic representation in film and media.
scoreSummary: Pillars Fund shows moderate impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Research published in partnership with Annenberg Inclusion Initiative and Ford Foundation

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 4.3% of revenue ($257,228 on $5,966,431)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fi_sabilillah (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 25/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; moderate program ratio (≥65%)
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.

components.name: Governance

judge_issues.message: Expenses ($4,783,807) exceed revenue ($2,398,957) by 99% - may be drawing down reserves

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: CEO compensation is 4.3% of revenue ($257,228 on $5,966,431)

risks.description: CEO compensation is 4.3% of revenue ($257,228 on $5,966,431)

score_details.score_summary: Pillars Fund shows moderate impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Pillars Fund is a grant-making organization and Muslim community foundation named in reference to the five pillars of Islam, the third of which is Zakat. Pillars is focused on funding secular non-profit programs supporting American Muslims in media, public relations, and leadership development. (Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pillars_Fund)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported a program expense ratio of 74.3%.
Pillars Fund reported total revenue of $5,966,431 and holds 20.7 months of working capital.
The mission of Pillars Fund is to strengthen relations with American Muslims and all Americans by supporting nonprofits that amplify Muslim talents.
The organization invests and leverages funds to support leadership and external affairs within the American Muslim community.
Pillars Fund produces multimedia publications and storytelling grants to shift cultural narratives.
The organization focuses on multimedia publications as a core programmatic output.
The organization provides various methods for general charitable giving and sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund maintains a moderate overall score due to its specialized focus on capacity building and cultural influence rather than direct social services, supported by healthy financial reserves.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact verification or standardized performance metrics
Moderate alignment with specific donor expectations regarding direct service delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 25/50, the fund offers a medium fit for donors interested in religious and cultural advocacy [3]. As a sadaqah-eligible organization, it provides a structured pathway for general charitable giving toward American Muslim civic participation [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a moderate impact score of 27/50, primarily functioning through capacity building and multimedia publications to influence public perception [5][7]. While it maintains a healthy program expense ratio of 74.3%, the lack of standardized cost-per-beneficiary data limits the assessment of direct effectiveness [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund empowers American Muslims by strategically investing in storytelling and leadership to reshape cultural narratives.
strengths: Strong financial liquidity with 20.7 months of working capital
High fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.12 to raise every dollar
Focused programmatic impact on cultural storytelling and capacity building
baseline_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund is a non-profit organization dedicated to strengthening relations between American Muslims and the broader public by amplifying Muslim talents and leadership [3]. Through initiatives like the Pillars Artist Fellowship and Storytelling Grants, the organization provides resources to creators and nonprofit leaders to ensure authentic representation [4][5].

all_citations.quote: To strengthen relations with American Muslims and all Americans.

all_citations.source_name: Pillars Fund - VP External Affairs JD

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund maintains a moderate overall score due to its specialized focus on capacity building and cultural influence rather than direct social services, supported by healthy financial reserves.

areas_for_improvement.detail: The organization has experienced a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite>, suggesting a need for more diversified or sustainable long-term funding streams.

While the organization has a <cite id="1">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, it currently lacks consistent <cite id="1">public beneficiary counts</cite> to assess cost-per-beneficiary metrics.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains high reserves (20.7 months) to mitigate the impact of revenue fluctuations.
risk_factors: <cite id="2">Revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite> over three years
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="2">significant revenue decline</cite> and a lack of <cite id="1">standardized impact metrics</cite> for their storytelling programs.

confidence.notes: Financial data is robust, but impact evidence is primarily self-reported.

alignment.explanation: The organization is a strong fit for <cite id="3">Sadaqah-based giving</cite> focused on American Muslim identity and <cite id="2">domestic civil society</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment for religious donors could be improved by <cite id="1">clearly stating the religious framework</cite> for their 'fi sabilillah' alignment.

credibility.explanation: Pillars Fund demonstrates transparency through its <cite id="1">Bronze Candid Seal</cite> and an <cite id="1">88% independent board</cite>. However, <cite id="2">officer compensation of $257,228</cite> relative to current revenue levels warrants closer donor scrutiny.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="1">publish consistent beneficiary data</cite> and move toward a higher Candid transparency seal.

impact.explanation: The organization is highly efficient, with a <cite id="1">74.3% program ratio</cite> and <cite id="4">$30 million in leveraged funds</cite>. It focuses on <cite id="4">capacity building</cite> rather than direct aid.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways for narrative shift.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Verified outcomes but limited cost-effectiveness data

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Strategic investment in storytelling and leadership capacity building

rich_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund empowers American Muslims by strategically investing in storytelling and leadership to reshape cultural narratives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term cultural infrastructure of American Muslims through storytelling and leadership development.
donor_motivations: Want to support authentic Muslim representation in media
Value strategic grantmaking over direct service delivery
Interested in domestic capacity building for Muslim nonprofits
Prefer supporting established institutions with high liquidity
giving_considerations: Focus is on long-term cultural shift rather than immediate humanitarian relief
Organization is currently navigating a period of revenue contraction
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, measurable humanitarian outcomes like meals or medical care.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified programmatic outputs like <cite id="5">multimedia publications</cite> but a lack of external impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Pillars Fund creates impact by funding Muslim creators and leaders to shift public narratives, thereby reducing marginalization.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in narrative change is difficult to measure but essential for justifying long-term investment in cultural work.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite high liquidity, the <cite id="2">sharp decline in revenue</cite> suggests a significant need for new donor support to maintain grant levels.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Unlike direct advocacy groups, Pillars Fund acts as a <cite id="2">grantmaker for 111 other organizations</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on direct community services in addition to arts
similar_organizations.name: Inner City Muslim Action Network

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on legal and policy advocacy
similar_organizations.name: Muslim Advocates

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on research and data regarding American Muslims
similar_organizations.name: ISPU

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">74.3% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that nearly all funds directly support storytelling and leadership initiatives.
With <cite id="1">20.7 months of working capital</cite>, Pillars Fund possesses the financial stability to sustain multi-year fellowships and grants.
Pillars Fund distributed <cite id="2">$6,560,000 to 111 domestic recipients</cite>, demonstrating its role as a central pillar of American Muslim civil society.

strengths_deep_dive: Pillars Fund distinguishes itself through its focus on cultural narrative change rather than direct social services. By producing <cite id="5">multimedia publications</cite> and supporting high-level media labs, they address the root causes of Muslim marginalization. Their <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised</cite> allows them to maintain a lean operation while managing a <cite id="2">board of 9 members</cite> with <cite id="1">88% independence</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund is a strategic grantmaking institution dedicated to <cite id="3">strengthening relations with American Muslims</cite> by investing in storytelling and leadership. The organization operates as a capacity-building hub, providing <cite id="4">Storytelling Grants and Artist Fellowships</cite> to ensure authentic representation of Muslim narratives in media and culture. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $2,398,957</cite>, a figure that reflects a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite> following a period of significant initial growth. Despite this contraction in revenue, Pillars Fund maintains a <cite id="1">strong program expense ratio of 74.3%</cite>, indicating that the vast majority of its spending is directed toward its core mission of cultural advocacy and grantmaking. The organization has <cite id="4">invested and leveraged over $30 million</cite> since its inception to support nonprofit leaders and creators. Its financial health is further bolstered by <cite id="1">20.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term strategic planning.
